backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor implementing clip management with CRUD operations, viral score calculation, similarity-based related clips, trending analytics, and auto-generation of clip suggestions based on engagement patterns.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting three implemented features: useAutoGenerateClips hook, Generate AI Clips button in VideoUrlForm, and App.tsx integration of automatic clip generation workflow.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Comprehensive summaries of all frontend files including components, hooks, and configuration, describing the React app structure with TanStack Router, clip management, AI suggestions, and admin panel.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom OKLCH color system defining design tokens for light and dark themes, including primary, secondary, muted, destructive, and chart colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point with root div and module script loading main.tsx, titled 'Beast Clipping'.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application with TanStack Router setup, managing video playback state, clip selection, AI-generated suggestions, and routing between home page and admin panel with permission checks.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React error boundary component that catches admin panel errors and displays a fallback UI with retry and home navigation options.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin dashboard displaying system statistics cards (total clips, trending clips) and a detailed trending clips analytics table with loading states, error handling, and retry functionality.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: Displays a single clip as a card with thumbnail, title, duration, viral score badge, and delete functionality with confirmation dialog. Supports selection state and click handling.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Renders a scrollable list of saved clips using ClipCard components, with loading and empty states. Manages clip selection and deletion through the useClips hook.
Displays either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending viral clips in a scrollable list. Each suggestion shows viral score, timestamp range, and a button to use the suggested clip.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Form component for creating clips with title input and start/end timestamp controls (minutes/seconds). Handles clip creation, validation, and loading suggested or selected clip data.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main layout wrapper with header (logo, title, admin link), footer with Caffeine.ai attribution, and sticky navigation. Conditionally shows admin link for owner users.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form for entering YouTube URLs with validation and video loading. Includes a button to trigger AI clip generation for the loaded video.
Badge component that displays a viral score (0-100) with color-coded styling and icons (flame/trending/sparkles) based on score thresholds. Supports multiple sizes and optional label.
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Embeds a YouTube video player in a responsive 16:9 aspect ratio card using an iframe with the YouTube embed API.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Manages the backend actor instance using React Query, creating authenticated or anonymous actors based on Internet Identity state and invalidating dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Fetches admin statistics including total clips count and trending clip analytics from the backend actor using React Query.
Provides mutation hook for auto-generating clip suggestions with mock AI-powered analysis (viral score, timestamps, titles) as a placeholder for future AI service integration.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Handles clip creation with a mutation that calculates viral scores based on duration and title characteristics, then saves clips via the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Fetches all clips and provides clip deletion functionality with loading state tracking using React Query mutations.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Manages clip timestamp state (start/end minutes/seconds), provides validation, formatting, and conversion utilities for time ranges.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides React context and hooks for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout flow, identity state, and AuthClient lifecycle with delegation validation.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: Checks if the current authenticated user is an admin by querying the backend actor with the user's principal.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: Fetches trending clips from the backend actor using React Query with automatic refetching when actor changes.
Provides utilities to validate YouTube URLs and extract video IDs using regex patterns.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light/dark theme colors using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query and Internet Identity providers, then renders the root App component.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom color scheme with OKLCH colors, container settings, animations, and plugin integrations for typography and animations.
project_state.json: Project state tracking file for a YouTube video clipping application called 'Beast Clipping' with 15 completed features including video URL input, embedded player, clip selection, AI suggestions, admin panel, and authentication. Contains a known issue that AI clip generation is listed as complete but not actually implemented.
